title: Koeleria pumila
intro: Koeleria pumila, also known as Dwarf Koelaria, is a small perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria pumila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Koeleria pumila is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal supplements and pharmaceuticals.
taxonomy: Koeleria pumila is classified under the kingdom Plantae, within the clade Angiosperms and further classified under the clade Monocots. It belongs to the family Poaceae and is classified as a type of grass. The genus Koeleria falls under the tribe Koelieae. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Koeleria pumila is also known to be a perennial grass species.
